data.title: Websea and Luffa have entered into a deep partnership to jointly explore new models for the integration of Web3 social and transaction ecosystems.
data.description: Digital asset trading platform Websea and Web3 creator and fan economy ecosystem application Luffa have officially entered into a deep partnership. The two companies will collaborate extensively across multiple dimensions, including traffic sharing, content co-creation, expansion of trading scenarios, and enhancement of commercial value, jointly exploring new models for the integration of Web3 social networks and digital asset trading. Websea will leverage Luffa's existing user base to gradually convert potential users into digital asset trading users; simultaneously, Websea's channel network will bring more new registered users to Luffa. At the product and feature level, Websea will also deeply integrate with Luffa to further expand trading scenarios.

data.title: Wallets associated with the Worldcoin team/investors deposited 4.63 million WLD tokens into Binance, resulting in a paper loss of $8.53 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet (0xDe2...fF2c) associated with the Worldcoin team/investors deposited 4.63 million WLD tokens, worth $1.25 million, into Binance. This address received these WLD tokens for $9.78 million a year ago and has now lost $8.53 million.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines as Bitcoin Outperforms
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index has decreased by three points, reaching a level of 34. According to NS3.AI, this index measures the performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies, excluding stablecoins and wrapped tokens, in comparison to Bitcoin over the last 90 days.

data.title: Solana App Founder Ben Pasternak Arrested on Assault Charges
data.description: Ben Pasternak, founder of the Solana ecosystem application Believe, has been arrested and charged with second-degree strangulation and third-degree assault, according to New York Criminal Court records. According to Foresight News, previous reports from SolanaFloor, citing the Australian Financial Review, indicate that Pasternak is facing charges in a New York court related to his alleged involvement in a crypto rug pull scheme, which resulted in millions of dollars in losses.

data.title: Believe founder arrested, faces second-degree murder and assault charges.
data.description: According to public records from the New York Criminal Court, Benjamin Pasternak, founder of Believe, has been arrested on charges of second-degree murder and assault and is scheduled to appear in court on June 11. He was previously accused of participating in a multi-million dollar token absconding scheme. The platform's native token, BELIEVE, is currently trading at approximately $0.00075, a drop of over 99% from its all-time high of $0.3569 in May 2025.

data.title: Solana Advisor Announces Changes to X Platform Features
data.description:
On April 23, Nikita Bier, the head of product for X and advisor to Solana, announced changes to two community organization features on the X platform. According to BlockBeats, the updates include enhancements to XChat and the discontinuation of X Communities.

XChat now supports group chats with joinable links, allowing users to create public links that can be shared directly on timelines. Each group chat can accommodate up to 350 members, with plans for further expansion.

Due to declining usage, the X platform will officially discontinue X Communities on May 6.

data.title: Circle initiated a proposal on the Aave forum to raise the maximum interest rate for USDC deposits to 48.2%.
data.description: Circle's Chief Economist, Gordon Liao, initiated a proposal at the Aave governance forum to urgently adjust the USDC interest rate parameters in the Aave V3 Ethereum main pool to address the pool's utilization rate, which has remained at an extremely high level of 99.87% for four consecutive days following the KelpDAO attack. Liao stated that Aave's current interest rate mechanism has failed to effectively "clear" the market. The pool currently has a supply of $1.89 billion and a borrowing amount of $1.89 billion, leaving less than $3 million in available liquidity. The borrowing rate remains at the upper limit after the inflection point (approximately 14%), and the pool size has shrunk by approximately $60 million in the past 24 hours—due to repayment funds being used to meet queued withdrawal requests. Therefore, the Slope 2 parameter in the USDC deposit rate curve should be immediately increased from approximately 10% to 40% through the Risk Steward mechanism, and the target should be further increased to 50% through a governance vote within 5 to 7 days. Meanwhile, the optimal utilization rate will be reduced from 92% to 87% during the transition phase, and further reduced to 85% after final confirmation. According to Liao's proposal, under the new parameter settings, when the utilization rate reaches 100%, the maximum deposit rate for USDC will increase from approximately 12.6% to 48.2%.

data.title: MetaMask co-founder Dan Finlay announced his departure from Consensys.
data.description: MetaMask co-founder Dan Finlay announced his departure from Consensys on April 23, ending over a decade of experience building MetaMask. He cited burnout as the reason for leaving, stating he plans to spend more time with his family. Finlay also expressed his satisfaction with the recently launched ERC-7715 advanced permissions feature on MetaMask, believing it fills a long-standing gap in the product.

data.title: Pornhub Switches Creator Payments from USDT to USDC
data.description:
Pornhub has transitioned from using USDT to USDC for creator payments. According to Foresight News, the change aims to enhance the reliability of earnings distribution. Pornhub informed creators via email that USDC is a fully reserve-backed stablecoin compliant with the EU's MiCA regulatory framework.

Previously, Pornhub adopted USDT in 2020 after ending its partnership with PayPal, utilizing the TronLink wallet for payment infrastructure. Following the switch, this collaboration has been removed from the creator page.

data.title: MetaMask Co-Founder Dan Finlay Departs Consensys After Over a Decade
data.description: MetaMask co-founder Dan Finlay has announced his departure from Consensys, effective April 23, after more than ten years with the company. According to Odaily, Finlay cited professional burnout as the reason for his decision and expressed a desire to spend more time with his family. He also expressed satisfaction with the recent introduction of the ERC-7715 advanced permissions feature in MetaMask, which he believes addresses a longstanding gap in the product.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft to Invest A$25 Billion in Australia by 2029
data.description: Microsoft has unveiled plans for its largest investment in Australia, committing A$25 billion ($17.9 billion) by the end of 2029. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the tech giant's strategic move to expand its presence in the artificial intelligence sector within the Asia-Pacific region. This investment underscores Microsoft's ambition to strengthen its foothold in AI, reflecting the growing importance of the technology in global markets.

data.title: U.S. Military Redirects Ships Amid Iran Blockade
data.description: The U.S. Central Command announced on April 23 that the U.S. military has redirected 29 vessels as part of its blockade operations against Iran. According to BlockBeats, recent media reports claimed that several commercial ships, including the 'HeroI,' 'Hedy,' and 'Dorena,' had evaded the blockade. However, these reports have been deemed inaccurate by the U.S. Central Command.

data.title: New Zealand Finance Minister: Unemployment Rate Could Reach 5.7% in Worst-Case Oil Price Scenario
data.description: New Zealand's Finance Minister has indicated that the Treasury forecasts a potential rise in the unemployment rate to 5.7% in the 2025/26 fiscal year if oil prices reach a worst-case scenario of $180 per barrel. According to Jin10, this projection is part of a broader analysis of the economic impacts of potential conflicts involving Iran, which could significantly affect global oil markets. The forecast underscores the potential economic challenges New Zealand may face if geopolitical tensions lead to substantial increases in oil prices.

data.title: AI TRENDS | SoftBank Seeks $10 Billion Margin Loan Using OpenAI Shares as Collateral
data.description: SoftBank is reportedly seeking a $10 billion margin loan, using its shares in OpenAI as collateral. According to Jin10, the Japanese multinational conglomerate is exploring this financial strategy to leverage its investment in the AI company. This move highlights SoftBank's continued interest in the artificial intelligence sector, as it aims to capitalize on the growing value of its stake in OpenAI. The loan, if secured, would provide SoftBank with significant liquidity, potentially enabling further investments or strategic initiatives in the tech industry. The development underscores the increasing importance of AI-related assets in global financial markets.

data.title: Strategy to Continue Bitcoin Investment Strategy, Says Michael Saylor
data.description: Strategy founder Michael Saylor announced that the company will persist in raising capital to acquire more Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Saylor emphasized that Strategy has no intention of diversifying into other assets despite the expansion of its Bitcoin holdings. He characterized Strategy as a pure-play Bitcoin stock, highlighting its focused investment approach.

data.title: One address withdrew 4,000 ETH from Kraken, worth $9.59 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address (0x66e...5b6b) withdrew 4,000 ETH from Kraken, with a total value of $9.59 million.

data.title: Legislative Challenges Surrounding the CLARITY Act
data.description:
Investment bank TD Cowen has highlighted several obstacles that could delay the legislative process of the CLARITY Act. According to Odaily, the Commodity Futures Trading Commission is currently understaffed, with only one commissioner in place, making it difficult for Congress to entrust more crypto regulatory responsibilities to the agency. Filling these positions could take months.

Additionally, the issue of prediction markets is gaining attention. The decision on whether to include them in the bill's regulations, along with concerns about insider trading and political conflicts of interest, including controversies related to projects associated with U.S. President Donald Trump, may lead some Democratic lawmakers to oppose the bill.

The ongoing controversy surrounding the Trump family's crypto project, World Liberty Financial, is also increasing the political sensitivity of the bill, making bipartisan consensus harder to achieve.

Geopolitical factors are also at play, with discussions about Iran potentially using crypto payments intensifying focus on anti-money laundering provisions, which could lead to amendments unfavorable to the industry.

Furthermore, some lawmakers are attempting to incorporate the Credit Card Competition Act into the legislation, which could spark new conflicts of interest and further hinder the overall legislative process.

data.title: Brent Crude Surpasses $100 per Barrel Amid Daily Increase
data.description: Brent crude oil prices have exceeded $100 per barrel, marking a daily increase of 3.30%. According to Odaily, this surge reflects ongoing volatility in the global oil market.

data.title: Tencent Cloud issued a risk notice regarding poisoning in the Xinference supply chain.
data.description: Tencent Cloud announced that its security center has detected a supply chain poisoning risk in Xinference. This risk could allow attackers to steal highly sensitive information such as cloud credentials, API keys, SSH keys, encrypted wallets, database credentials, and environment variables when users install or import affected versions of the package, and then send this information to a remote command and control (C2) server. (Jinshi)

data.title: SBF withdrew its motion for retrial, stating that it could not obtain a fair trial from a judge.
data.description: SBF has sent a letter to New York Judge Lewis Kaplan announcing the temporary withdrawal of his Rule 33 motion for a retrial. SBF stated that he decided to withdraw the motion because he needed to focus on answering the judge's questions regarding his lawyer's involvement in drafting the motion, and because he believed he could not obtain a fair trial before the judge. He plans to reserve the right to resubmit the motion after a ruling on his direct appeal and request for a reassignment judge. Previously, SBF was sentenced to 25 years in prison for defrauding clients and investors. In the letter, he confirmed that the motion was primarily drafted and researched independently by him while in a Brooklyn jail, with his parents only providing editorial advice and assistance with printing.
